subject: Databases
title: Backward-Compatible Changes
description: Learn Backward-Compatible Changes in one minute.

hook.text: Deploy a new schema, but the old app version is still running. If the schema breaks the old code, users see errors.

buildup.text: Backward-compatible changes: add columns, add tables, add indexes. These don't break existing queries.

discovery.text: Breaking changes: rename columns, change types, drop columns. Old code that references them fails.

twist.text: The expand-contract pattern: add the new column, migrate data and code, then remove the old column in a later deploy.

climax.text: Blue-green deployments need backward-compatible schemas. Both old and new code must work with the same database.

punchline.text: Add first, migrate, then remove. Never break what's running.

quiz.question: What is the expand-contract pattern for schema changes?
options: Add the new structure, migrate data and code, then remove the old structure in a later deploy
Expand the table size and then contract it to compress data
Run two databases simultaneously and merge them later
